signals.title: USD/JPY — Geopolitical + Carry
signals.text: Middle East escalation drives safe-haven demand — JPY COT LF net short −65,309 (crowded carry). USD/JPY at 159.62; 160.00 is the key level that has previously triggered BoJ verbal warnings; intervention risk rises on a sustained break above 160. Risk-off flow historically unwinds JPY shorts — 157.00 is the first technical level on a sustained risk-off move.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Positioning + Vol + Retail
signals.text: AUD/USD COT LF net long +41,675 vs retail 89% short — institutional vs retail divergence at extreme. HV30 at 6.8%, ATM IV (FXE chain) at 8.2% — options pricing a 1.4 vol-pt premium over realized. 25d RR at −0.21: mild put skew despite the bullish price trend — market hedging rather than chasing. Late-trend caution signal. A LF delta turning positive or retail capitulation would invalidate the caution read.

signals.title: EUR/USD — CB + Technical
signals.text: ECB 2% vs Fed 3.75% — 175bp EUR carry disadvantage; EUR/USD structurally capped. EUR LF net short −72, long%=50.0% — neutral positioning. ATM IV 8.5% near HV, 25d RR flat — options neutral. EUR/USD at 1.1705 mid-range; 1.1720 is the next resistance on a continuation.

signals.title: GBP/USD — Positioning + Vol
signals.text: GBP/USD COT LF net long +28,426, WoW delta +5,321 — increasing long positioning. HV30 at 7.5%, ATM IV (FXE chain) at 9.5% — elevated volatility. 25d RR at +0.15: mild call skew — bullish price trend. GBP/USD at 1.3487; 1.3600 is the next resistance level.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT + OIS
signals.text: NZD/USD COT LF net short −17,090 vs RBNZ OIS hike 100% priced — specs fading a hawkish CB. If RBNZ delivers or signals hike, short squeeze risk is elevated. NZD/USD at 0.5892; 0.5900 is the next technical level.
